description: TypeScript-specific rules from common_rules.md TypeScript Standards section

rules.name: no_any_type
rules.description: Avoid the 'any' type — it defeats the purpose of TypeScript
rules.message: Explicit 'any' type used. Replace with a specific type or 'unknown' with a type guard.
rules.fix_suggestion: Use a specific interface/type, or 'unknown' + type narrowing: if (typeof x === 'string') { ... }

rules.name: type_function_params_and_returns
rules.description: Function parameters and return types must be explicitly typed
rules.message: Function is missing a return type annotation.
rules.fix_suggestion: Add return type: function myFn(param: string): void { ... }

rules.name: prefer_undefined_over_null
rules.description: Prefer undefined over null per team coding standard
rules.message: null value used. Prefer 'undefined' over 'null' per TypeScript standards.
rules.fix_suggestion: Replace null with undefined unless interoperating with APIs that require null.
rules.comment: Disabled — too many false positives with API responses. Enable if your team enforces this strictly.

rules.name: use_id_not_ID
rules.description: Use 'Id' suffix (not 'ID') for identifiers per coding standards
rules.message: Identifier uses 'ID' suffix. Use 'Id' instead (e.g., userId not userID).

rules.name: interface_or_type_for_props
rules.description: React component props should be typed with an interface or type alias
rules.message: Component props are not typed via an interface or type alias.
rules.fix_suggestion: Define: type MyComponentProps = { title: string }; const MyComponent = ({ title }: MyComponentProps) => ...

rules.name: no_non_null_assertion_abuse
rules.description: Excessive use of ! (non-null assertion) bypasses type safety
rules.message: Non-null assertion (!) used. Add a proper null check instead of asserting non-null.
rules.fix_suggestion: Use optional chaining (?) or an explicit if-check before accessing the property.
